rulings.Case Title: VATCHE CABAYAN M.D. VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 24, 2012, Line 3, CROSS DEFENDANT VATCHE CABAYAN M.D.'s DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled. Defendant does not need to exhaust an administrative remedy under Vacanti and Labor Code 5304. Judicial estoppel does not apply because defendant's positions were not inconsistent and defendant was not successful in its prior argument. The RICO and breach of contract causes of action are adequately pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS GAMACHE et al VS. NICK VOLENTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Prime Pacific Investments, Inc. Joinder With Melvin M Moran'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Danielle Mcgee To Attend And Testify At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 24, 2012, Line 1. Defendant Prime Pacific Investments, Inc.'s Joinder With Melvin M Moran'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Danielle Mcgee To Attend And Testify At Deposition. Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. Newly added defendants have a right to take a full deposition of the plaintiff pursuant to CCP §2025.210, et seq. Appear for argument on sanctions request. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELICA M. GONZALEZ VS. ALFONSO PICAZO, AS TRUSTEES OF THE ARTHUR AND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To The Second Affirmative Defense Of Defts Answers; Memo Of P&A; Request
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 24, 2012, Line 8, PLAINTIFF ANGELICA GONZALEZ's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To The Second Affirmative Defense Of Defendants Answers. Granted. Although accounting is pled as a separate cause of action, it is in effect a request for relief incidental to the partition cause of action. Therefore, the statute of limitations for partition causes of action controls. There is no statute of limitations on partition causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELICA M. GONZALEZ VS. ALFONSO PICAZO, AS TRUSTEES OF THE ARTHUR AND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 24, 2012, Line 8, PLAINTIFF ANGELICA GONZALEZ's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied and the alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues one, two, four, five, six and seven and granted as to the third issue. Plaintiff failed to shift the burden on the first cause of action because she does not address all of the breaches of contract alleged in the Third Amended Cross-Complaint. The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues one, two, four, five, six and seven because they are not proper issues to be determined by summary adjudication. An issue of breach is not properly determined on summary adjudication, and determining one of several issues of breach would not dispose of either the first or second cause of action in its entirety. As to the third issue. Cross-Defendant shifted the burden and Plaintiff failed to raise a triable issue of fact. Because the third issue only addressed the breach of oral contract allegations in the Third Amended Cross-Complaint, the motion is granted only as to the breach of oral contracts. (Lilienthal & Fowler v. Superior Court, (1993) 12 Cal.App.4th 1848.) The breach of written contract allegations remain. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CCP § 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR BIOLOGICAL DIVERSITY, VS. CALIFORNIA FISH AND GAME COMMISSION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 24, 2012, Line 9, PETITIONER CENTER FOR BIOLOGICAL DIVERSITY's Petition For Writ Of Mandate. Denied. The Commission's interpretation of "range" in Fish and Game Code section 2062 is correct. In reaching its decision, the Commission used the best scientific information available. Substantial evidence supports the Commission's findings supporting its decision to not list the Fisher as an endangered or threatened species. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JOHN T. PRIEST VS. STARBUCKS CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 24, 2012, Line 26, DEFENDANT STARBUCKS CORPORATION DBA STARBUCKS COFFEE COMPANY's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied as to the request for punitive damages and granted as to the allegations regarding defendant's remedial measures. Paragraph 9 sufficiently alleges that defendant acted willfully and with a conscious disregard for plaintiff's safety. The allegations regarding defendant's remedial measures are irrelevant within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure section 436 because they do not support the negligence/premises liability cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DENTPRO FRANCHISE CORP., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. BRAD EVANS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 24, 2012, Line 3. PLAINTIFF DENTPRO FRANCHISE CORP., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied. The Court finds counsel for defendant inadvertently missed the deadline to respond to the request for admissions pursuant to C.C.P. §2033.300. Counsel are to appear regarding the request for sanctions and be prepared to discuss why monetary sanctions would be unjust. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RALPH JACOBSON VS. CHRISTIANE HAYASHI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 24, 2012, Line 21, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. San Francisco Transportation Code § 1122(g) is subject to the rational basis test. The ordinance furthers the valid legislative purposes of customer convenience, customer safety, and the avoidance of customer confusion. The ordinance does not violate principles of equal protection or due process. The ordinance is not arbitrary even though other cash-based industries are not subject to the same requirement. (Berry v. Hannigan, (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 587, 592). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC. A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Strike Portions; Req For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 24, 2012, Line 23, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Strike Portions. Denied. The determination of what is reasonable time to make a demand for payment is a factual question to be made on the particular circumstances of each case. The language of paragraph 100A indicates that defendant is responsible for arrears throughout the term of the contract and paragraph 7 of the complaint alleges that the term of the contract was extended. Given these facts, the Court cannot conclude what, if any, claimed damages are time-barred. While paragraph 12 does not appear to be an attorney's fees clause, the clause is ambiguous and it would be premature to strike the prayer at this stage of the litigation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN MAYO et al VS. CADELINIA & LEUNG, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 24, 2012, Line 24, DEFENDANT D.P.P.M., INC. DBA ZEPHYR REAL ESTATE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second cause of action for negligent misrepresentation and overruled as to the third cause of action for negligence and fourth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty. Plaintiff needs to plead the negligent misrepresentation claim with more particularity as to this defendant. The third and fourth claims are adequately alleged and defendant may not demurrer to part of a cause of action. rulings.Case Title: TRIMARK ERF, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. BLMG INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION DBA GEORGES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Applciation And Hearing For Right To Attach Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 24, 2012, Line 9. PLAINTIFF TRIMARK ERF, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order. Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted as unopposed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)
